name: Limit Scope of Shared Data
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 13: Concurrency
problem: Shared mutable data spread across many classes or methods is accessed and mutated by multiple threads, causing race conditions, data corruption, and non-deterministic bugs that are extremely hard to reproduce and fix.
steps: Identify all data fields accessed by more than one thread.
Reduce the number of places that read or write shared data to an absolute minimum.
Use the synchronized keyword, locks, or concurrent data structures only around those minimal access points.
Prefer passing copies of data between threads instead of sharing a reference to a mutable object.
Consider using immutable objects for shared data; immutability eliminates the need for synchronization.
